data.label: Seed a synthetic NAS
data.description: Writes a small, deterministic file tree into /tmp/mekhan-crawl-demo (cleaning any prior run first) so the rest of the workflow has something real to crawl WITHOUT touching a production file server. ~7 files across two subdirectories (datasets/, plots/, logs/). Outputs the root path and the file count.

data.label: Crawl the file tree (file-ops crawl)
data.description: The file-ops `crawl` operation: a recursive, streaming, checkpointable directory walk under the `mekhan-crawl-demo/` prefix of the local `/tmp` storage root (i.e. /tmp/mekhan-crawl-demo, the dir seed_nas just wrote). Unlike `list` (which buffers the whole Vec inline), crawl drives the OpenDAL streaming lister and `stat()`s each entry for size+mtime, emitting fixed-size `{path,size,mtime}` batches over the `crawl` control channel (batch_size 3 here, so the ~7 files arrive across several batches). It reads no file bytes — metadata only; integrity hashing is the later step's job. The scalar outputs (count/last_path/batches) summarize the walk; the per-file rows ride the channel for the consumer to fold.

data.label: Fold the crawl batches
data.description: Consumes the crawl step's `crawl` control channel with `join: gather`, so the compiler's gather barrier re-orders the emitted batches by index and parks the whole collection as `{output: [<batch>, …]}` — the inbound `input` token. Each batch is a `{items: [{path,size,mtime}, …]}` dict; this step flattens them into one `{path, size}` list and counts the files. This is the consumer-side fold: the producer just emitted a uniform stream, the join discipline lives on the edge.

data.label: Register each file BY REFERENCE (log_artifact upload=False)
data.description: Registers each crawled file BY REFERENCE via `log_artifact(..., upload=False)` — no bytes are moved, no HTTP is made. The runner is co-located with the files, so for each folded file the SDK/executor hashes it locally over IPC and uploads NOTHING (the bytes stay on the file server). A single `log_artifact` call is the whole registration: the platform's causality projector fills BOTH halves atomically server-side — the content-addressed `catalogue_entries` row (logical identity, keyed on the SHA-256) AND the physical `file_inventory` copy at `(file_server_id, reference_path)`. The literal `fold.files`, `seed.root`, and `start.file_server_id` reads in the source MUST stay verbatim so the compiler synthesizes their read-arcs.
